_corrections.reason: Leaf-checked (scan n1): the record is the 31 Dec 1794 Argüello Monterey cuenta at codex pp.1-2; Bancroft's footnote (HoC i.367, context 1792) cites St. Pap., Sac. i.115-17 — wrong attachment in the census run. Suspects pending HoC-context check: ca6-d150 ('xi.160,195-6'), ca12-d9 ('xix.113-16').

Leaf verification 2026-07-12 (scans n250/n284/n372/n374/n375/n452): C-A 25's leaves carry TWO page systems — original tomo pages (what the gap-read recorded as orig_page) and handwritten per-tomo leaf numbers (what Bancroft's footnotes cite). All 21 census attachments for this volume were page-joins across mismatched systems and are suspended until the volume's re-verification (queued). Case study: 'Prov. Rec., MS., ix. 122-3' (HoC II:90 n.21) had attached to the 1804 otter/presidio docs (d4/d6/d7/d8); the true document is Arrillaga's 28 Jun 1810 Suisun-campaign report at tomo leaves 122-123 = scans n374-375, now ca25-d37. Also: 24 records were mislabeled Tomo IX (fixed to X); scan links run ~2 behind BookReader positions; Tomo VIII (scans n0-n249) is uncataloged.

_corrections.detail: Those records are physically Tomo V (title leaf n402, 'Vol. V Military & Political 1780-1821', found via the Berkeley digicoll crosswalk F34) but were cataloged as IV with Tomo V's restarting original pagination — so Bancroft's iv.N cites joined them by page coincidence. Content-verified true targets in the real Tomo IV: iv.1->d4001 (1792 church-work order), iv.5->d4004 (1785 twins pardon), iv.30-1->d4020 (1782 Neve Reglamento approval), iv.69->d4035 (1796 rains), iv.74-7->d4038 (1796 Goycoechea). Re-join correctly at the Q9 census rebuild.
